trivia_bullets.text: Warded Battlements was released in Core Set 2021 (M21), a standalone core set released July 3, 2020, designed as an entry-point set for new players. M21 featured 274 cards and included reprints alongside new cards, with a core-set emphasis on clear, readable mechanics.

Illustrated by Titus Lunter, a Dutch concept artist specializing in environmental and architectural design, known across MTG for his many land arts and fortress/structure illustrations. Lunter has contributed to MTG since 2014.
sources: WebSearch: Titus Lunter MTG artist profile

trivia_bullets.text: A Creature — Wall with Defender and a passive '+1/+0 to attacking creatures you control' aura — an unusual pairing: a wall that buffs attackers. Legal in Modern, Pioneer, Historic, Commander, and Pauper. EDHREC rank 22,147 places it firmly in fringe/casual territory.
"In dire times, even a place of refuge can become a weapon of war." The flavor text frames the card's mechanical paradox directly: a defensive structure that empowers offensive action.

related_cards.name: Stalwart Shield-Bearers
related_cards.note: Earlier Wall with a similar 'attacking creatures get +0/+1' clause; structural-buffer concept parallel

related_cards.name: Angelic Wall
related_cards.note: Recurrent M-series white Wall at similar cost; comparison benchmark

related_cards.name: Wall of Omens
